summary: postinstall script exfiltrates host metadata to webhook.site
details: On `npm install`, the package runs `postinstall.js` (declared via `scripts.postinstall: node postinstall.js`), which executes the `id` shell binary via `execSync` and collects the installer's hostname, platform, architecture, current working directory, Node version, and a timestamp. The collected JSON payload is POSTed via `https.request` to the hardcoded URL `https://webhook.site/fceebb0d-9f11-4ac0-98db-6f6b3925f7d3` (postinstall.js line 14). The exfiltration fires unconditionally during default install with no opt-out. Although the package self-describes as a proof-of-concept, the install-time data flow to an attacker-controlled webhook endpoint is real and produces concrete installer harm: any machine running `npm install` on this package leaks UID/GID, hostname, and working-directory context to a third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
